title: Defined medium for Neocallimastigomycota
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2c4a","text":"This is the protocol for the completely defined medium base for Neocallimastigomycota (anaerobic gut fungi, AGF) that was used within the HiPoAF and FUNGAS projects. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5b5bh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54h9r","text":"If you add 2 g/L xylan and 3 g/L cellobiose as C-sources to this medium, Neocallimastigomycota - in our experience - can grow on it for prolonged periods (more than 18 months and running, with weekly sub-cultivation). We use this medium, however, more for short-term experiments rather than long-term culture maintenance. So far, we have tested this medium on strains of Anaeromyces mucronatus, Caecomyces communis, Neocallimastix cameroonii, Neocallimastix frontalis, Pecoramyces ruminantium and all were able to grow. However, depending on the C-source added, they might show unusual clustering behaviour (e.g., clinging onto the glass). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":371,"length":22},{"style":"italic","offset":395,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":416,"length":25},{"style":"italic","offset":443,"length":24},{"style":"italic","offset":469,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ddltg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rr5j","text":"This completely defined medium remains nearly colourless if no C-source is added before autoclaving the bottles, which is especially convenient for microscopy. For this purpose, a soluble C-source can be added directly to the bottles after autoclaving. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9b6c3","text":"Before you start, make sure you have prepared all necessary stock solutions, and have all chemicals needed at hand (see Materials section for details). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5h24i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d2t8l","text":"You will need approx.   in total to prepare the medium (depending on the volume you prepare, it can take longer or might be faster). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":22,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2okt3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b8mph","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"a1ppi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"duration","mutability":"","data":{"duration":7200,"guid":"95DFA840829A11ECB445A952F44B4781","id":0,"label":"","pause":null,"preset":[],"start":null}},"1":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"8flt","text":"Please also ensure, that all necessary safety measures are in place (see also: Safety Warnings). ","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"1":{"type":"safety","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"7v42a","text":"Be also aware of the risks of autoclaving closed serum bottles, and make sure your autoclave has an appropriate program for that. ","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}
materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"b4tsg","text":"Here we list the ingredients of our minimal medium for AGF cultivation and the preparation of stock solutions needed for this medium: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":133}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7b8vb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8prut","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3td80","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"16tg3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3uenh","text":"Hemin solution is prepared by dissolving   hemin powder in a 1:1 mixture of 96% ethanol and   NaOH. The solution is then filter sterilzed (0.22µm pore size) and stored at   until use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":41,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":92,"length":1},{"key":3,"offset":171,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2870o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"421ep","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"639ur","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ago65","text":"Resazurin solution is prepared by dissolving   resazurin powder in MilliQ water and filter sterilizing it (0.22µm pore size). The solution is then stored at   until use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":45,"length":1},{"key":5,"offset":157,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7v7oq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2336e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a698h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e2i9m","text":"Salt Solution I consists of   K2HPO4 dissolved in MilliQ water. It is autoclaved ( ,  ), and stored at   until use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16},{"style":"sub","offset":31,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":35,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":6,"offset":28,"length":1},{"key":7,"offset":82,"length":1},{"key":8,"offset":85,"length":1},{"key":9,"offset":103,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1hirj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ap1m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dqobu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1evpl","text":"Salt Solution II consists per litre of   KH2PO4,    (NH4)2SO4,   NaCl,  MgSO4.7H2O, and   CaCl2.2H2O.  The ingredients are dissolved in MilliQ water one after the other. The solution is then autoclaved ( ,  ), and stored at   until use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17},{"style":"sub","offset":43,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":46,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":55,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":57,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":60,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":76,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":80,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":94,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":98,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":10,"offset":39,"length":1},{"key":11,"offset":50,"length":1},{"key":12,"offset":63,"length":1},{"key":13,"offset":71,"length":1},{"key":14,"offset":88,"length":1},{"key":15,"offset":203,"length":1},{"key":16,"offset":206,"length":1},{"key":17,"offset":224,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"a2mll","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ups","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4fo04","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cs50d","text":"       Ingredient         g/L            CoCl2.6H2O         0.05            CuSO4         0.021            FeSO4.7H2O         0.20            H3BO3         0.25            MnCl2.4H2O         0.25            Na2MoO4.2H2O         0.28            Na2SeO3         0.04            NaVO3         0.03            NiCl2.6H2O         0.25            ZnSO4.7H2O         0.026      ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":18,"offset":0,"length":371}],"data":{}},{"key":"cffgf","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3v9bb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cd9s0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4a1b2","text":"PAS-212 antibiotic stock solution is prepared by slowly dissolving   Streptomycin sulfate,   Penicillin G sodium salt, and   Ampicillin sodium salt in distilled water. The mixture is then gassed with pure CO2 for up to  , then closed with a rubber stopper and crimped to obtain and maintain anoxic conditions. With the help of a needle and syringe, the solution is filter sterilized (0.22µm pore size) into a sterile, anoxic, closed & crimped serum bottle and stored at   until use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33},{"style":"sub","offset":207,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":19,"offset":67,"length":1},{"key":20,"offset":91,"length":1},{"key":21,"offset":123,"length":1},{"key":22,"offset":219,"length":1},{"key":23,"offset":470,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"83n75","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fv0jo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7vfqj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dh4r","text":"       Biotin         0.2            Calcium Pantothenate         0.6            Folic acid         0.05            Niacin         0.6            Nicotinamide         1.0            p-Aminobenzoic acid         0.05            Pyridoxamine         0.1            Riboflavin         0.2            Thiamine hydrochloride         0.01            Vitamin B12 (Cyanocobalamin)         0.02      ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":24,"offset":0,"length":390}],"data":{}},{"key":"1fh7u","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1jg1k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"491la","text":"Serum bottles. For standard AGF cultivation, we usually use glass serum bottles with an ND20 head and a total of volume of 120 mL. In those serum bottles, we do not add more than 50 mL medium, to ensure an adequate amount of head space. Alternatively, smaller serum bottles with a total volume of 60 mL also work (filled with no more than 30 mL of medium). We recommend using the bulky serum bottles rather than the long, thin ones because the longer, thinner ones tend to easily break and are less convenient when handling the bottles. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bspqs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"o5qr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"atgq9","text":"Rubber stoppers. The bottles are usually closed with 3-legged butyl rubber stoppers or with full-body black rubber stoppers and crimped with a metal cap.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2asbu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{},"colTitles":["Ingredient","Amount per litre"],"colWidths":["240",100],"data":[["Hemin Solution","2 mL"],["L-cysteine-HCl","1 g"],["MilliQ water","700 mL"],["Resazurin Solution","2 mL"],["Salt Soluion I","150 mL"],["Salt Solution II","150 mL"],["Sodium hydrogen carbonate (NaHCO3)","6 g"]],"guid":"51AD2C40827911ECB445A952F44B4781","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"1lob4","text":"Ingredients of 1 litre of completely defined medium. This medium contains no C-source, so be sure to add the appropriate one for your experiments! For minimal visual disturbance, you can add 3 g/L cellobiose or 6 g/L glucose (useful in microscopy, for example). Complex, insoluble C-sources (e.g. wheat straw) should be added directly into the serum bottles (before adding the medium), at a concentration of around 0.35 g / 50 mL. steps: Add salt solution I, salt solution II, hemin solution, resazurin solution, and MilliQ water to a cooking pot. If you want to use xylan as a C-source (or other C-sources that don't dissolve well in water), you should add it here as well.

Heat the mixture and simmer until everything is dissolved and the colour starts to change to yellow. For us with 2 L of medium in a 5 L cooking pot, this takes about 00:20:00 . Take care not to cook it too long. Otherwise, you will lose a lot of water, making the medium more concentrated. You can also microwave it carefully instead of cooking it in a pot.
Carefully pour the mixture from the pot into the Erlmeyer flask. Cool it with ice-water while agitating it by magnetic stirrer and bubble it with pure CO 2 . This takes about 00:10:00 to 00:20:00
While the mixture is heating up, flush a large enough Erlmeyer flask with pure CO 2 for 00:02:00 .

Once the mixture has cooled to around 39 °C, add the trace elements solution, NaHCO 3 , soluble C-source (like cellobiose, glucose, etc.; if necessary) and lastly the L-cysteine-HCl. Bubble with CO 2 for another 00:10:00 .
Set the pH of the medium to pH 6.9 with the help of NaHCO 3 powder or 5M NaOH solution. The medium should now be a translucent yellowish colour.
While setting the pH, flush your serum bottles with pure CO 2 for 1-2 minutes, to minimize oxygen contamination when aliquoting your medium in step 6.

Aliquot the medium into your serum bottles under continuous flow of CO 2 in the serum bottles and the Erlmeyer flask filled with medium. Depending on the amount of medium prepared this step takes around 00:30:00 . Dispension of medium into 120mL glass serum bottles (max. 50mL per bottle). The medium depicted is not the medium described in this protocol. The process, however, is the same. Picture copyright: Julia Vinzelj & Nico Peer

Take the gas hose out of the serum bottle and immediately close it with a rubber stopper, then crimp the bottle with an aluminium cap. The medium can turn slightly pink at first upon transfer, but this usually settles quickly. If the medium turns bright pink upon transfer, however, keep bubbling it with CO 2 within the serum bottles for up to 00:05:00, min before closing it up. Closing of the serum bottles with 3-legged butyl-rubber stoppers after the medium has been dispensed. The medium depicted is not the medium described in this protocol. The process, however, is the same. Picture copyright: Julia Vinzelj & Nico Peer.
Autoclave the serum bottles at 121 °C for 00:20:00 .
Store the bottles without regard to temperature for up to 2 months. 4 °C works as well as 37 °C for the duration of 2 months at least. Discard any bottles with medium that turned pink or red.

Before use, the bottles should be warmed to 39 °C . Also, 0.01mL vitamin solution must be added per mL of medium shortly before use by injection. If needed, a sterilized solution of soluble C-source (e.g. glucose, cellobiose), and antibiotic stock solution can be injected as well. Injection of Vitamin Solution into sterile, pre-warmed serum bottles shortly before use. Picture copyright: Julia Vinzelj & Nico Peer Colours of the completely defined medium. From left to right: medium turned pink (should not be used; indicates oxygen contamination); sterile medium with no C-source added (we call it "Brunhilde" in our lab, because we are incredibly funny and working with us in the lab is a blast); sterile medium with vitamin solution and glucose solution added after autoclaving; sterile medium with xylan and cellobiose added before autoclaving (known to us as "Agathe"); sterile medium with only wheat straw as C-source (our "Berthelm"). Picture copyright: Julia Vinzelj
